name: North West Housing Partnership
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor providing free home repair assistance, affordable housing development, and homebuyer education across Chicago's northwest suburbs since 1990.
description_long:
North West Housing Partnership (NWHP) is a nonprofit organization established in 1990 that operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency across the Chicago metropolitan area. The organization focuses on creating economic diversity in housing through public-private partnerships and serves low to moderate-income residents throughout Cook, DuPage, McHenry, and Kane counties. NWHP operates as a catalyst connecting public and private resources to implement community housing initiatives.

NWHP offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost housing services including home repair programs, handyman assistance, the CAPABLE (Community Aging in Place Advancing Better Living for Elders) program for seniors 60+ and people with disabilities, affordable rental housing developments for seniors 55+, homebuyer education workshops, and housing counseling. The organization also manages home repair programs on behalf of the Illinois Housing Development Authority (IHDA) and multiple municipalities. Their CAPABLE program specifically addresses accessibility repairs in nine defined service areas across northern Illinois suburbs.

The organization distinguishes itself through its dual focus on direct resident assistance and community-level housing development. NWHP actively develops and preserves affordable housing units while simultaneously providing individualized support services. The organization maintains HUD approval for housing counseling, administers programs for state housing authorities, and explicitly commits to serving clients with greatest economic and social need, including minority populations and those with limited English proficiency. Their 25+ year track record demonstrates sustained impact across multiple counties.

NWHP is genuinely free for eligible low-to-moderate income residents, with no hidden fees or credit requirements. The primary limitation is geographic—services concentrate in northwest Chicago suburbs and specific townships. Consumers outside their service area cannot access their programs. Additionally, home repair assistance appears limited to minor repairs and handyman work rather than major renovations, and some programs require age or disability qualification.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency with official government designation
Completely free services for eligible low-to-moderate income residents—no hidden fees
CAPABLE program offers free in-home repairs and accessibility modifications for seniors 60+ and people with disabilities
Administers programs for Illinois Housing Development Authority and multiple municipalities, ensuring programmatic legitimacy
Explicit commitment to serving minority populations and non-English speakers with greatest economic need
Develops and preserves affordable rental housing while providing direct resident assistance
Established 1990 track record with 25+ years serving thousands across four counties
cons: Geographic service limitation: only serves specific northwest Chicago suburbs and designated townships in Arlington Heights, Buffalo Grove, Mount Prospect, Palatine, Rolling Meadows, Des Plaines, Oak Park area
Home repair assistance appears limited to minor repairs and handyman work, not major renovations or comprehensive rehabilitation
CAPABLE program restricted to residents 60+ or adults 18+ with disabilities; unable to help working-age adults without disabilities
Limited information on website about eligibility income thresholds or application process specifics
Handyman program availability may be limited by funding and volunteer/contractor resources
best_for: Seniors 60+ or people with disabilities in northwest Chicago suburbs seeking free home repairs and accessibility modifications
Low to moderate-income first-time homebuyers in northern Illinois suburbs seeking free counseling and educational workshops
Renters and homeowners with limited incomes seeking affordable housing options in Cook, DuPage, McHenry, and Kane counties
diagnosis: NWHP is best for low-income seniors, people with disabilities, and first-time homebuyers specifically located in northwest Chicago suburbs seeking free, legitimate HUD-approved housing counseling and repair assistance. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—if you live outside their defined service areas (primarily northwest Chicago suburbs and specific townships), their programs are unavailable to you.
services: Home repair programs administered through partnerships with IHDA and municipalities
CAPABLE (Community Aging in Place Advancing Better Living for Elders) program with free repairs for seniors 60+
Handyman services for seniors and people with disabilities
Affordable rental housing development for seniors 55+
Public and private partnership facilitation for community housing initiatives
Free educational resources and program guides
Accessibility modification assistance for aging in place
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1701 E Woodfield Rd Ste 203, Schaumburg, IL 60173
